top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 90, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Punta del Este's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
5,423ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
60ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Punta del Este. Train it until it feels boring.
GFNY Punta del Este's flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 90 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,423ft of climbing on flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 90 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At GFNY Punta del Este, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 90 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
The aerobic base that lets you ride flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped at tempo for 90 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Punta del Este’s 90 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 90 miles of flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped drama in Punta del Este, Maldonado Department, Uruguay.
